steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtracting the second parentheses changes both signs inside it. The $6c^3$ term becomes negative, and subtracting $-7$ adds $7$. After distributing the subtraction, the expression can be simplified by combining only like terms.
$$(5c+8)-(6c^3-7)=5c+8-6c^3+7$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Combine the constants and write the terms in standard order.
$$5c+8-6c^3+7=-6c^3+5c+15$$
The answer is B.
